title: evolvulus fuscus
intro: Evolvulus fuscus, also known as dwarf blue-dust or blue eyes, is a flowering plant species in the family Convolvulaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Evolvulus fuscus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Evolvulus fuscus are typically dried and then used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
